title: Protium palmeri
taxonomy: Protium palmeri is classified as a species of plant in the family Burseraceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the torchwood or copal family. This classification places it within the order Sapindales, a larger group that includes various families of flowering plants. Protium palmeri is a member of the genus Protium, which comprises several species of trees and shrubs native to tropical regions. The family Burseraceae is a relatively small group of plants that are primarily distributed in tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Within the genus Protium, Protium palmeri is one of several species that are native to specific regions, including Baja California in Mexico.
uses: Protium palmeri is a plant species native to Central and South America. Protium palmeri has been traditionally used by indigenous communities for its medicinal properties. The plant's bark and roots are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems. The plant is also used to treat wounds and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Further research is needed to confirm the full range of medicinal uses and potential benefits of Protium palmeri.

side_effects: Protium palmeri, commonly known as palo santo, is used in traditional medicine. However, there is limited information available about its side effects.
